capability: structural-reveal discrimination — isolate a line whose bond joins two previously-separate regions of the entity graph
claim: A bond between two STANDING entities sitting in separate graph components is a structural reveal; the interior connectivity channel (bridgeSurprise) fires maximally on it and ~0 on surface-matched controls (adjacent re-bond, fresh entity), while the mass-based one-surprise (bayesBits) does NOT isolate it (ranks fresh-mass lines above it); the connectivity reading is invariant to a surface emphasis cue.
stimulus_shape: text · discrimination+invariance · L3 · whole-reading · target CON
verdict: CONFIRMED — controls dark (adj=0, fresh=0), bridge unique-argmax on the reveal (1.0), bayes argmax elsewhere (gap real), surface-invariant (unmarked==marked)
mechanism: connectivity collapse over the CON/SIG bond graph: a bond's endpoints in different components -> 1, same-component geodesic delta -> (delta-1)/Dinf, adjacent -> 0; endpoints resolved through the CAUSAL SYN-merge identity quotient (the engine's own union-find, restricted to sentIdx<=cursor) so a reveal that names a standing entity by a new surface form is seen
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new src/core/bridge.js, exported from src/core/index.js beside surpriseAt; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js as an OPT-IN channel readingAt({bridge:true}) so the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: the mass one-surprise (surprise.js) has no atom for connectivity — a bond among standing entities is a tiny mass deposit; the structural convergence leaves no trace in the figure field. The existing diagnostic (scripts/reveal-discrimination.mjs bridgeSurprise) located this but lived only in the read-only harness AND keyed on raw surface ids, so it MISSED short-name reveals and faked a 2-hop separation on adjacent controls.
parity: npm test 649->658 (9-test lock added), default readingAt byte-identical (bridge opt-in)
generalization: 194/194 lines of the original reveal-stimulus.json reproduce the prior raw-id bridge column EXACTLY (no regression); coref-resolution is a strict superset — identical where reveals use full names, strictly better on short-name reveals
omnimodal: CONFIRMED on a pure-operator log (no text/parser): cross-component bond -> 1.0, adjacent -> 0, SYN-coref reveal -> 1.0 — the mechanism reads only operators, so any organ emitting CON/SIG/SYN inherits it
scope_holds: any modality whose organ emits bonds (CON/SIG) and identity merges (SYN); structural reveals that join graph regions; reveals naming a standing entity by a new surface form
scope_fails: a reveal carried by NO bond (a pure predication/contradiction leaves connectivity unchanged — the enacted significance loop's province); convergence the weak SVO parser does not draw as a bond or shared object (front-end limit, not the channel's)

capability: novelty-rate-sensitive reserve — surprise at a genuine newcomer tracks how often newcomers have RECENTLY arrived, not a fixed constant
claim: The reserve mass a reading holds for an as-yet-unseen atom should be a signal-derived amplitude: the γ-decayed recent newcomer rate, run through the FIXED Born step. So a newcomer after a long stretch of pure confirmation (rate→0, reserve→0) is far more surprising than the identical-surface newcomer in a churn of newcomers (rate high, reserve high). The fixed-constant reserve 1/(mass+1) is blind to the rate and scores the two ~equally; the dissociation is newcomer-specific (a repeat-final control stays flat under both reserves).
stimulus_shape: text+tone · discrimination + constant-hunt · L3 significance · across-a-window · target EVA/the reserve
verdict: CONFIRMED — signal SEPARATES (settled−churning = 1.48 bits at len10, 2.25 at len15) while fixed is BLIND (Δ=0.00, the loud-surface control); control flat (|Δ|=0.07); monotone in confirmation length; omnimodal text≡tone byte-identical (17/17 score, 12/12 lock)
mechanism: signal-derived novelty reserve: noveltyReserveMass folds the γ-decayed first-appearance steps of the prior atoms (the recent newcomer rate, under the SAME γ the figure field decays by) and feeds it as the `novelty` AMPLITUDE through the unchanged Born step (reserve = a/(sum+a)) inside surpriseAt. Context enters at the amplitude; the law stays put. Applied to both reserves reading.js holds — the proposition-field reserve (surpriseAt/forwardDist) and the figure-field reserve (Z/pNovel).
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new noveltyReserveMass in src/core/surprise.js, exported from src/core/index.js; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js behind RULES_REV (env) / opts.signalReserve, default OFF so every existing path is byte-identical. surpriseAt (the Born law) is unchanged.
root_cause_of_gap: NOVELTY_RESERVE=1.0 is a hand-rolled constant: under a steady deposit rate sumPrior saturates (Σγ^k→1/(1−γ)), so 1/(sumPrior+1) saturates to a near-constant — the reserve is a function of accumulated mass, blind to whether newcomers are actually arriving. The reader fails to learn the novelty rate from its own signal. The constant was the teacher the self-learning system is not supposed to have, smuggled in as a literal.
parity: npm test 676→688 (12-test lock added); default readingAt byte-identical (live churning-newcomer value pinned at 0.28); RULES_REV=1 flips the live path (0.28→1.56) verified at the trace level; exp-0001 structural-reveal re-run CONFIRMED (bridge independent, default bayes column unchanged)
generalization: the fix lifts BOTH key conditions across TWO senses (4 newcomer probes + 4 length variants), not one planted pair; the control proves it is newcomer-specific, not a context artifact; breaks no confirmed capability (exp-0001 green, full suite green)
omnimodal: CONFIRMED in two senses — the text organ (parsed entities) and a non-text tone organ (a bare INS operator-log, no parser) produce the IDENTICAL dissociation (1.56/0.08 both), because the mechanism reads only the membrane (first-appearance steps + decay). A fix that helped only text would have leaked; this cannot — it never sees a modality.
scope_holds: any modality whose organ streams arrivals onto the log (the reserve over any γ-decayed basis profile); the significance (bayes) channel and the predictive (figure-field) channel alike; discriminating a newcomer's surprise by the recent novelty rate
scope_fails: behind RULES_REV (not yet default-on, pending broader golden promotion across the 202 snapshots); the empty-prior opening falls back to a unit reserve (provably score-invariant there); the rate is a count of FIRST-appearances, so it does not yet distinguish a slow drift of the generating distribution from discrete newcomers (the drifting-signal hard problem — the next pressure this opens)
